# Phase 8 Catastrophic Survivability Plan

This planning note records Phase 8.5 scope for deterministic recovery-state and catastrophic survivability architecture. It is documentation-only and cannot create executable workflows.

## Scope

- catastrophic recovery doctrine
- deterministic restoration pathways
- governance continuity during failure
- infrastructure collapse containment
- survivable degraded-state operations
- constitutional continuity during catastrophe
- recovery lineage continuity
- fail-closed disaster handling
- infrastructure resurrection governance
- post-failure auditability doctrine
- recovery blockers and escalation

## Out Of Scope

- runtime authorization
- provider use
- provider adapters
- executable workflows
- deployment automation
- autonomous orchestration
- infrastructure activation
- infrastructure activation paths
- disaster-automation execution systems
- authorization-capable runtime logic
- credential authority
- secret authority
- deployment authority
- execution authority
- runtime activation states
- recovery authorization

## Completion Criteria

- Recovery processes cannot authorize runtime.
- Restoration pathways cannot activate providers.
- Catastrophic modes cannot bypass governance supremacy.
- Degraded-state operation cannot enable execution authority.
- Recovery sequences cannot bypass human review.
- Resurrection governance cannot create deployment authority.
- Recovery doctrine cannot enable autonomous orchestration.
- Catastrophic handling cannot override constitutional continuity.
- Disaster response cannot create execution-capable infrastructure.
- Restoration sequencing cannot bypass fail-closed doctrine.
